Click on shapes to remove them from the game, causing the remaining pieces to fall and settle according to realistic physics. Complete all 100 levels by clearing every red shape without letting any green shape fall off the platform. Carefully plan your moves - you have limited clicks per level! Automatically click on random shapes at regular intervals (every 60 frames). The test will remove shapes in a randomized pattern to verify that the physics engine correctly handles shape removal and that remaining shapes settle naturally under gravity. Shapes should disappear when clicked, and remaining shapes should fall and settle realistically. The physics simulation should remain stable without errors. The test is successful if shapes can be removed without crashes and the game continues to function normally. Testing win condition by strategically removing only red shapes. This verifies that the game correctly detects when all red shapes are removed and all green shapes remain on the platform, triggering the level win state. Systematically identify and click only on red shapes, starting from the top of the structure and working downward. Wait for physics to settle between clicks to ensure green shapes remain stable. Continue until all red shapes are removed. After all red shapes are removed and green shapes remain stable on the platform, the game should transition to the win state. The level complete screen should appear, showing the option to proceed to the next level. Test is successful when the win condition is properly detected. Testing lose condition when green shapes fall off the platform. This verifies that the game correctly detects when green shapes leave the safe zone and triggers the game over state appropriately. Deliberately click on shapes that support green shapes, causing them to fall off the platform. Target shapes at the bottom or edges that, when removed, will cause green shapes to tumble off the designated area. When a green shape falls below the platform or off the screen, the game should detect the failure and transition to the game over lose state. The game over screen should display the appropriate failure message. Test succeeds when the lose condition is properly triggered. Testing click limit enforcement and game over when clicks are exhausted. This verifies that the game properly tracks the number of clicks used and triggers game over when the player runs out of clicks with red shapes still remaining. Click on random shapes until the click limit is reached, intentionally avoiding complete removal of all red shapes. Use up all available clicks before achieving the win condition. When clicks are exhausted and red shapes remain on screen, the game should transition to game over lose state. The game should display a message indicating the player ran out of clicks. Test is successful when click limits are enforced correctly. Testing level progression and difficulty scaling. This verifies that levels advance properly, that level data is loaded correctly, and that difficulty increases as expected through the progression system. Complete multiple levels in sequence by removing red shapes efficiently. After each win, proceed to the next level and verify that new shape configurations load properly with appropriate difficulty adjustments. Levels should advance smoothly, with each new level presenting a different configuration. The level counter should increment correctly, and shapes should spawn in new patterns. Test succeeds when at least 5 consecutive levels can be completed without errors. Testing pause and restart functionality. This verifies that game state is properly preserved during pause, that the pause overlay displays correctly, and that the restart function returns the game to the start screen properly. During gameplay, press ESC to pause, verify the game stops updating, then unpause and continue. Unpausing should resume from the exact same state. Restarting should reset all game variables and return to the start screen. Test succeeds when state transitions work correctly. Remove shapes and then wait for an extended period to ensure the physics simulation fully settles. Monitor the game's stability detection to confirm it correctly identifies when all shapes have come to rest. Red Wrecker

Red Wrecker is a physics-based puzzle game where you must eliminate all red shapes while keeping all green shapes safely on the platform. Click on shapes to remove them from the game, causing the remaining pieces to fall and settle according to realistic physics. Complete all 100 levels by clearing every red shape without letting any green shape fall off the platform. Carefully plan your moves - you have limited clicks per level!

Controls: MOUSE CLICK: Remove a shape | ENTER: Start the game | ESC: Pause/unpause | R: Restart | SPACE: Next level (when won)

TEST_6.test_description: Testing pause and restart functionality. This verifies that game state is properly preserved during pause, that the pause overlay displays correctly, and that the restart function returns the game to the start screen properly.
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Pausing should freeze all physics and gameplay, displaying a pause overlay. Unpausing should resume from the exact same state. Restarting should reset all game variables and return to the start screen. Test succeeds when state transitions work correctly.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing shape stability detection and physics settling. This verifies that the game correctly determines when shapes have stopped moving and are stable, which is crucial for detecting win/lose conditions accurately.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Remove shapes and then wait for an extended period to ensure the physics simulation fully settles. Monitor the game's stability detection to confirm it correctly identifies when all shapes have come to rest.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: After shape removal, the game should wait for all shapes to stop moving before checking win/lose conditions. Shapes should be considered stable when their velocities drop below a threshold. Test succeeds when the game doesn't prematurely check conditions while shapes are still moving.
